sections.paragraph:
, מְלֹוא once מְלֹו Eze 41:8, m.

(1) fulness (Fülle); hence that which is full; followed by a genit. 1Ki 17:12, מְלֹא כַף “fulness of hand,” i.e. a handful, eine Hand voll. Exo 9:8, מְלֹא חָפְנֵיכֶם “the fulness of your hands,” i.e. your hands full, Hände voll. Also, with the addition of the thing with which any thing is full. Num 22:18. Jud. 6:38 , מְלֹוא הַסֵּפֶל מַיִם “a basin full of water.”- Amo 6:8, עִיר וּמְלֹאָהּ “the city and those who fill it;” i.e. its inhabitants. Isa 42:10, וּמְלֹאֹו אִיִּים וְישְׁבֵיהֶם הַיָּם “the sea and those that fill it (i.e. sailors, as it were the inhabitants of the sea), the islands and their inhabitants.” Psa 96:11. Hence-

(2) multitude, crowd; Arab. مَلَاءُ, Syr. ܡܠܘܘܐ in Barhebr. often. Gen 48:19, מְלֹא הַגֹּויִם. Isa 31:4.
